popularStory.description: Margaret Beauchamp (c. 1410 – before 3 June 1482) was the daughter of Sir John Beauchamp, de jure 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, and his second wife, Edith Stourton. She was the maternal grandmother …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset於1405年1月1日出生在英國貝德福爾布勒左時，她的父親Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe23歲，母親Edith Stourton26歲。她於大約1424年與Sir Oliver St John在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1482年8月8日在英國多瑟特維波內 明斯特享年77歲，埋葬在英國多瑟特維波內 明斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset于1405年1月1日出生在英国貝德福爾布勒左时，她的父亲Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe23岁，母亲Edith Stourton26岁。她于大约1424年与Sir Oliver St John在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1482年8月8日在英国多瑟特維波內 明斯特享年77岁，埋葬在英国多瑟特維波內 明斯特。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset was born on 1 January 1405, in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, her father, Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, was 23 and her mother, Edith Stourton, was 26. She married Sir Oliver St John about 1424, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 8 August 1482, in Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset est née le 1 janvier 1405, à Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Edith Stourton, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Oliver St John environ 1424, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 août 1482, à Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset am 1. Januar 1405 in Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe 23 und ihre Mutter Edith Stourton 26. Sie hat um 1424 in England Sir Oliver St John geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. August 1482 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England und wurde in Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset è nata il il 1 gennaio 1405 nel Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, England, sua padre, Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Edith Stourton, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir Oliver St John circa 1424, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 8 agosto 1482, nel Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolta a Wimborne Minster, Dorset, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somersetが1405年1月1日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアブレトソで生まれた時，父Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoeは23歳，母Edith Stourtonは26歳であった。約1424年にイギリスでSir Oliver St Johnと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1482年8月8日にイギリスドールセトウィンボルン ミンスターで77歳で死去し，イギリスドールセトウィンボルン ミンスターにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset(이)가 1405년 1월 1일 영국 베드퍼드실르 블레츠에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Edith Stourton(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1424년 영국에서 Sir Oliver St John(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1482년 8월 8일 영국 도르세트 위본 민스터에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 도르세트 위본 민스터에 묻혔다.

pt.1 January 1405–8 August 1482: 1 de janeiro de 1405–8 de agosto de 1482
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1405, em Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Edith Stourton, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Oliver St John aproximadamente 1424, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de agosto de 1482, em Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Inglaterra, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset родилась 1 января 1405 г. в местности блецо, бедфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Edith Stourton, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Oliver St John около 1424 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 8 августа 1482 г. в местности вимборн минстир, дорсет, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности вимборн минстир, дорсет, Англия.

es.1 January 1405–8 August 1482: 1 de enero de 1405–8 de agosto de 1482
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baroness Margaret Beauchamp Duchess of Somerset nació el 1 de enero de 1405, en Bletsoe, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Beauchamp 3rd Baron Beauchamp of Bletsoe, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Edith Stourton, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir Oliver St John aproximadamente 1424, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1482, en Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Wimborne Minster, Dorset, Inglaterra.
